Petition For Order Affirming Construction Of Trust Instrument; Construing Trust Provision; Or Modifying Trust
Off Calendar Jul-07-2025 Continued To Sep-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-19-303148 The James Won Ling Bong Revocable Trust,PES-19-303149 Estate of James Won Ling Bong and PTR-23-306828 The Fortune 8 Trust.

Appearances: Audrey Grossman appeared by zoom for Betty Bong and Annie Tang (co-trustees).
Robert Cross appeared in person for Mary Bong (petitioner/respondent).
Anita Rana appeared by zoom for Annie Tang (administrator).
Kevin Rodriguez appeared by zoom for Annie Tang and Betty Bong (individuals).
Jane Hong (Certified Court Reporter) CSR-11975 csrjane@hotmail.com (909-831-2644).

Discussion was held regarding the third amended petition filed to add more assets, to include seven properties in the real estate holding firm James Bong Enterprise, Inc., that discovery has been ongoing but there has not been any discovery on the new claims, and that Mr. Cross is requesting to file a motion regarding the 5-year statue.

The Court authorizes counsel to file a Motion Tolling the 5-year Statute of Limitations set for August 7, 2025 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204.

All matters are continued to September 10, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A Joint Status Statement is due one week prior to the hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Reported by Jane Hong, CSR-11975 csrjane@hotmail.com (909-831-2644).

Petition For Order Affirming Construction Of Trust Instrument; Construing Trust Provision; Or Modifying Trust
Off Calendar May-05-2025 Continued To Jul-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-19-303148 The James Won Ling Bong Revocable Trust,PES-19-303149 Estate of James Won Ling Bong and PTR-23-306828 The Fortune 8 Trust.

Appearances: Audrey Grossman appeared by zoom for Betty Bong and Annie Tang (co-trustees).
Robert Cross appeared in person for Mary Bong (petitioner/respondent).
Anita Rana appeared by zoom for Annie Tang (administrator).
Lisa Petak appeared by zoom for Annie Tang and Betty Bong (individuals).
Emily Foehr appeared via zoom for objectors.

Discussion was held regarding the newly amended petition alleging that the decedent signatures on certain documents were for forged by Annie, Betty and Attorney Pung, that Court did receive the joint status report filed on April 29, 2025, and that the Court does permit the filing of third amended petition. Mr. Cross stated that he has depositions scheduled in early June and will need a 60 day continuance. Ms. Grossman said she is ready for trial. The Court stated that it would like to have all petitions tried together. All matters are continued to July 7, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An amended status report is due one week before the hearing date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Thompson. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PTR-19-303148, PES-19-303149 and PTR-23-306828

Appearances: Audrey Grossman appeared via zoom for Betty Bong.
Emily Foehr appeared via zoom for objectors.
Kevin Rodriguez appeared via zoom for respondents.
Robert Cross appeared via zoom for Mary Bong.

Discussion was held regarding outstanding discovery, that they plan on taking a few more depositions. Amended Petition for Approval of First Account etc., is taken under submission. All other matters are continued to January 27, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court orders an updated joint status report.
.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Wong. Not reported.

Petition For Order Affirming Construction Of Trust Instrument; Construing Trust Provision; Or Modifying Trust
Off Calendar Jun-10-2024 Continued To Sep-23-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-19-303148 The James Won Ling Bong Revocable Trust,
PES-19-303149 Estate of James Won Ling Bong and PTR-23-306828 The Fortune 8 Trust.

Appearances: Audrey Grossman appeared by zoom for Betty Bong and Annie Tang (co-trustees).
Robert Cross for Mary Bong (petitioner/respondent).
Kyla Hale appeared by zoom for Annie Tang (administrator).
Azita Rahim appeared by zoom for Way Pung (third party deponent).
Kevin Rodriguez appeared by zoom for Annie Tang and Betty Bong (individuals).

The parties reported that they are still in the process of conducting discovery, that there is extensive law and motion practice in this case, that there is a pending unlawful detainer action, and that they agree to continuing the matter as they are not ready for trial. The Second Amended Petition for Orders Directing Conveyance or Transfer of Property to Probate Estate, etc., the case management hearing, the 850 Petition for Order Confirming Trust Assets, and the Petition for Order Affirming Construction of Trust Instrument, etc., are continued to September 23, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An updated joint status report is to be filed one week prior to the hearing. The parties are to try and take care of all discovery and any other filings.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Malone/Santos. Not reported.

Petition For Order Affirming Construction Of Trust Instrument; Construing Trust Provision; Or Modifying Trust
Off Calendar Feb-07-2024 Continued To Mar-11-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Production Of Trust Insturment
Off Calendar Jan-07-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Shearer for Mary Bong (Petitioner).
Catherine Swafford for Annie Tang and Betty Bong (Respondents).

Mr. Shearer stated that he still wants an order compelling production of the trust, that he has received it, that it was done two weeks after filing of the petition and that he does not trust the Respondents. The Court stated that it will not issue an order when the issue is moot and that the trust has already been produced. Mr. Shearer stated that the amount of the costs he is seeking is $500.00. Ms. Swafford stated that her Clients object to costs being awarded, that the Decedent died on June 3, 2019 in Shanghai, China, that her Clients were in China and did not immediately accept the office of Trustees, that they took office in September, that notice was provided within 60-days and that the petition was unnecessary. Ms. Swafford also stated that this is not simple matter, that the Decedent had assets all over the world, that her Clients acted reasonably and in good faith, and that she can file a supplement as to when her Clients took the position as trustees. The Court noted that Counsels' hourly rate for today's appearance exceeds the $500.00 amount, and gave the option of continuing the matter for filing of an objection and a response, or the parties stipulating to the representation of Ms. Swafford as to when her Clients took trusteeship. Counsel agreed to stipulate. The Petition to Compel Production of Trust Instrument is under submission. Each side is to provide a proposed order. The Court informed Counsel that the order on the issue of costs will fully dispose of the petition.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
